title: ZUNION
description: Returns the union of multiple sorted sets.
syntax:
ZUNION numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS weight [weight ...]]
  [AGGREGATE <SUM | MIN | MAX>] [WITHSCORES]
complexity: O(N)+O(M*log(M)) with N being the sum of the sizes of the input sorted sets, and M being the number of elements in the resulting sorted set.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information. This command is similar to [`ZUNIONSTORE`](), but instead of storing the resulting sorted set, it is returned to the client. For a description of the `WEIGHTS` and `AGGREGATE` options, see [`ZUNIONSTORE`](). ## Examples ZADD zset1 1 "one" ZADD zset1 2 "two" ZADD zset2 1 "one" ZADD zset2 2 "two" ZADD zset2 3 "three" ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the result of the union with, optionally, their scores when _WITHSCORES_ is used. **RESP3:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the result of the union with, optionally, their scores when _WITHSCORES_ is used.
intro:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.



This command is similar to [`ZUNIONSTORE`](), but instead of storing the resulting
sorted set, it is returned to the client.

For a description of the `WEIGHTS` and `AGGREGATE` options, see [`ZUNIONSTORE`]().

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
This command's behavior varies in clustered Redis environments. See the [multi-key operations]() page for more information.



This command is similar to [`ZUNIONSTORE`](), but instead of storing the resulting
sorted set, it is returned to the client.

For a description of the `WEIGHTS` and `AGGREGATE` options, see [`ZUNIONSTORE`]().

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
ZADD zset1 1 "one"
ZADD zset1 2 "two"
ZADD zset2 1 "one"
ZADD zset2 2 "two"
ZADD zset2 3 "three"
ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2
ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the result of the union with, optionally, their scores when _WITHSCORES_ is used.

**RESP3:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): the result of the union with, optionally, their scores when _WITHSCORES_ is used.
